_comment: Per-action guidance injected into the conv_template when trigger=yes. The generator uses these to produce conversations whose ending naturally implies the target action without explicitly naming it.

check_weather.description: Suggest the user check the weather forecast.
check_weather.when_to_suggest: The perspective person has an upcoming outdoor or weather-sensitive plan and the conversation ends without anyone resolving the weather question.
natural_signals: anticipating outdoor activity (picnic, hike, beach, BBQ, walk, run, outdoor wedding, festival)
questions about what to wear / what to pack
uncertainty about umbrella / jacket / sunscreen
planning travel to a region with different climate
anti_signals: the plan is indoors (weather is irrelevant)
someone already shared a forecast in the conversation
the event is months away
check_weather.example_ending_pattern:
B: 'okay see you saturday at the trailhead 9am 🌲'
A: 'sounds good!! hope the rain holds off'

open_maps.description: Suggest opening a maps app for directions or a location lookup.
open_maps.when_to_suggest: The perspective person is heading to a specific place (especially unfamiliar) and the conversation establishes the location near the end without anyone sharing a route.
natural_signals: address dropped at the end of the conversation
uncertainty about how to get somewhere ('where exactly is this place?')
ETA estimation requested
first-time visit to a venue / neighborhood / unfamiliar city
anti_signals: they've been there many times
the other person already shared a pin or maps link
the meeting is at someone's well-known home
open_maps.example_ending_pattern:
B: '7pm at Tatsu Ramen — 2700 W Main St'
A: 'on my way!'

order_food.description: Suggest opening a food-delivery app or restaurant menu.
order_food.when_to_suggest: Hunger or a meal decision is the live topic near the end and no one has placed an order yet.
natural_signals: 'i'm starving', 'what's for dinner?', 'order something?'
discussing cravings or restaurant choices
post-workout / late-night hunger
kids asking what's for dinner
anti_signals: they're cooking / already at a restaurant / just ate
someone said 'I'll order' (use action_completed_in_convo instead)
order_food.example_ending_pattern:
B: 'okay i'm DONE for the day, dinner??'
A: 'starving fr. thai or pizza?'

send_gift.description: Suggest sending a gift via a gifting app or e-commerce site.
send_gift.when_to_suggest: An imminent occasion calling for a gift is mentioned and the perspective person hasn't bought anything yet.
natural_signals: someone's birthday this week / next weekend
anniversary / housewarming / promotion / baby shower / graduation
wanting to thank or congratulate someone
condolences (sympathy flowers)
anti_signals: they've already bought it (action_completed_in_convo)
the gift logistics are someone else's responsibility (other_party_action)
send_gift.example_ending_pattern:
B: 'mom's bday saturday btw 🎂'
A: 'ah!! ty for the reminder'

send_money.description: Suggest opening a P2P payment app (Venmo, Cash App, Zelle, Apple Cash).
send_money.when_to_suggest: The perspective person owes someone money or has agreed to pay them and the conversation ends before they've sent it.
natural_signals: splitting a bill / Uber / shared cost / utilities / rent
owing for a meal / ticket / favor
explicit 'i'll send you' / 'lmk what i owe'
covering for someone temporarily
anti_signals: they already sent it (action_completed_in_convo)
the OTHER person owes them (flip to other_party_action)
send_money.example_ending_pattern:
B: 'no worries lol your share was $32'
A: 'ahh ok ty! 🙏'

set_reminder.description: Suggest setting a calendar reminder / alarm / notification.
set_reminder.when_to_suggest: A specific upcoming time/date commitment is made by the perspective person near the end of the conversation, especially when the person is conversational/forgetful.
natural_signals: agreeing on a meeting time / appointment / call
asking to be reminded of something
important deadline / call / pickup time mentioned
promising to do something at a specific future time
anti_signals: the time is hours away and obvious / routine
the OTHER person will handle the timekeeping
set_reminder.example_ending_pattern:
B: 'cool — call at 3pm tomorrow?'
A: 'perfect, talk then'

share_photo.description: Suggest opening the photo library or camera to send an image.
share_photo.when_to_suggest: The perspective person is asked to send a photo (existing or to-be-taken) and hasn't yet.
natural_signals: 'send me a pic'
'do you have a photo of that?'
request for proof / show & tell
wanting to share a meal / event / pet / outfit / haircut / kid
anti_signals: they already sent it (action_completed_in_convo)
the OTHER person is the one being asked to share
share_photo.example_ending_pattern:
B: 'wait i need to SEE this dress'
A: 'omg ok hold on'

take_note.description: Suggest jotting something to a notes app.
take_note.when_to_suggest: Information worth remembering is given to the perspective person (recipe, address, password, tip, idea, name) and they haven't saved it.
natural_signals: a recipe / measurements shared
a useful phone number, address, or code received
a recommendation or idea they should remember
to-do list items dictated
anti_signals: they already wrote it down (action_completed_in_convo)
the info is already saved (e.g., in a contact)
take_note.example_ending_pattern:
B: 'recipe: 2 eggs, 1 cup flour, 1/2 cup milk, bake at 375 for 20'
A: 'ohhh perfect ty'

web_search.description: Suggest opening a browser / web search.
web_search.when_to_suggest: A question of fact comes up that the perspective person doesn't know the answer to, isn't going to learn from this conversation, and would naturally want to look up.
natural_signals: uncertainty about a fact ('what year was that?', 'who plays them?')
wanting to verify a claim
a name / product / place / movie / book mentioned that they're not familiar with
looking up information for a decision (e.g., 'is that restaurant any good')
anti_signals: the other person provides the answer
they already looked it up (use already_resolved_elsewhere)
web_search.example_ending_pattern:
B: 'apparently the new bukowski biography is incredible'
A: 'oh huh, who's it by again?'
